resources.string: Scan · Dial · Done — works with no internet
Point at any UPI QR code
Your data never leaves this phone
No internet needed · No UPI PIN in this app
1. Scan the shop\'s UPI QR with your camera.
2. Check the name and amount on the big screen.
3. Tap Pay — your phone dialer opens *99#.
4. Follow the steps and enter your UPI PIN in your bank\'s screen.
This app never sees your UPI PIN. Payment happens on your bank\'s secure *99# service.
Hold the QR code inside the box
Camera permission is needed to scan QR codes.
Camera was not allowed. Enable it in Settings to scan.
The name and UPI ID look different. Make sure you know this shop before paying.
First time paying this UPI ID. Confirm it belongs to the right shop.
You have paid this UPI ID before.
This is not a valid QR code.
This is not a UPI QR code.
This QR has no UPI ID.
The UPI ID in this QR is not valid.
The amount in this QR is not valid.
This amount is above the ₹5,000 *99# limit. Pay a smaller amount.
Only Indian Rupee (INR) payments are supported.
Enter a valid amount between ₹1 and ₹5,000.
Your dialer will open *99#. Do this:
4. Enter your UPI PIN to finish
Copied — you can paste it in the *99# box
You have two SIMs. In the dialer, pick the SIM linked to your bank account.
No phone dialer found on this device.
After you finish, your bank sends an SMS confirmation. OruBar Pay never sees your PIN.
No payments yet. Your history stays only on this phone.
Stored on this phone only — UPI ID, amount and time. No PIN, no account number.
It reads a shop\'s UPI QR code and opens your phone\'s *99# service so you can pay with no internet. The payment happens on your bank\'s secure service — this app only helps you get there.

OruBar Pay hands over to your bank\'s *99# service. If it fails: check you used the SIM linked to your bank, that you have basic mobile signal, and that your bank supports *99#. Try again after a moment.
*99# only works from the SIM number that is registered with your bank. When the dialer asks which SIM, choose that one.
Jio: use the SIM registered with your bank; *99# works over the mobile network, no data needed.
Airtel: dial *99# and follow the menu; works at low signal.
Vi (Vodafone Idea): dial *99#; if it times out, wait and retry.
BSNL: reliable for *99# even at one bar of signal.
Yes. You enter your UPI PIN inside your bank\'s *99# screen, never inside OruBar Pay. This app has no PIN box and cannot go online.
